label: Threat Intelligence Analyst
description: Collects, analyzes, and disseminates threat intelligence to inform defensive security decisions. Covers TTP analysis, threat actor profiling, IOC management, MITRE ATT&CK application, and intelligence reporting for technical and executive audiences.

paths.name: Commercial CTI Track
paths.rationale: The most recognized combination for commercial CTI teams, MSSPs, and threat intelligence vendors. GCTI is the benchmark credential; supplementing with platform-specific certifications builds operational tool fluency.
pros: GCTI is the most recognized vendor-neutral CTI credential globally
MISP provides practical threat sharing platform skills valued in CERT/CSIRT environments
Strong combination for both in-house CTI teams and intelligence vendors
cons: GCTI MCQ format limits demonstration of actual analytical tradecraft
High cost without SANS training sponsorship
MISP has limited recognition outside CERT/CSIRT and European markets

prerequisites.experience: SOC analyst or security operations experience is strongly beneficial. CTI is a mid-career specialization — foundational security operations exposure first is recommended.

cert_sequence.notes: The primary CTI cert. Open-book — build comprehensive indexed notes organized by intelligence cycle phase, MITRE ATT&CK concepts, and threat actor profiling methodology. Associated with SANS FOR578. Without SANS training use the free MITRE ATT&CK training, Mandiant threat intelligence reports, and the SANS Cyber Threat Intelligence Summit talks available on YouTube. GCTI genuinely requires understanding analytical tradecraft — it cannot be pure memorization.

CyberDefenders' practical CTI certification covering IOC collection, threat intel platforms, OSINT, and intelligence sharing frameworks. 80% practical weight makes this a useful hands-on complement to GCTI's MCQ format. Builds the platform operation and IOC management skills that day-to-day CTI work requires. CyberDefenders platform is well regarded for realistic lab scenarios.

Free MISP platform certification covering event management, IOC sharing, taxonomy, and STIX/TAXII. Particularly valuable if your organization participates in threat sharing communities (ISACs, national CERTs). Even where MISP itself isn't deployed, the threat sharing framework knowledge transfers to other TIPs. An easy add-on that costs nothing and demonstrates platform operation skills.

paths.name: Government / DoD Track
paths.rationale: For CTI analysts targeting government, intelligence community, or cleared contractor positions. GCTI provides DoD 8140 coverage; CREST CIP adds senior government intelligence credibility for UK and allied-nation positions.
pros: GCTI is DoD 8140 approved for cyber intelligence roles
CREST CIP is the most rigorous CTI credential with significant practical weight
Strongest path for senior government intelligence analyst roles
cons: CREST CIP primarily recognized in UK, Australia, Singapore, EU — limited US recognition

prerequisites.experience: Prior intelligence analysis or security operations experience is expected. CREST CIP targets senior practitioners with demonstrable analytical experience.

cert_sequence.notes: The DoD-approved foundation. Build thorough indexed notes. GCTI alongside Security+ satisfies most government CTI analyst role requirements at the mid-level.

CREST's advanced CTI credential — the most rigorous and practically weighted intelligence certification available. Significant written and practical components covering strategic intelligence, attribution, collection management, and intelligence product development for senior stakeholders. Targets senior practitioners with substantial CTI experience. Preparation requires real analytical work, not just study — your GCTI foundation and operational CTI experience are both essential inputs.

paths.name: Budget CTI Track
paths.rationale: arcX FTIA provides a free CTI foundation from a CREST-accredited provider. arcX PTIA covers the full CREST CPTIA syllabus at ~$380 — the most affordable CREST-aligned CTI preparation available. MCTIA adds a genuinely proctored Mandiant-branded credential at $250. Total under $650 — the most accessible credentialed CTI path before committing to GCTI.
pros: arcX FTIA is free — zero cost entry point
arcX PTIA covers the full CREST CPTIA syllabus — CREST-aligned at fraction of CPTIA cost
MCTIA is a genuinely proctored, renewable cert with Mandiant/Google Cloud brand recognition
Under $650 total — the cheapest credentialed CTI path available
cons: arcX credentials are course-completion, not externally proctored
Lower HR brand recognition than GCTI or CREST CPTIA
MCTIA MCQ-only — no practical tradecraft component
Path assumes self-study for analytical methodology beyond course content

prerequisites.experience: No prior CTI certs required. SOC or security operations exposure beneficial.

cert_sequence.notes: arcX Foundation Level Threat Intelligence Analyst — free, ~2.5 hours, covers CTI fundamentals and intelligence lifecycle. CREST-accredited training provider. Start here to validate interest in CTI before investing further. Complete the free CTI 101 course and pass the embedded final exam.

arcX Practitioner Threat Intelligence Analyst — ~£300 (~$380), 17+ hours covering the full CREST CPTIA syllabus. The most affordable CREST-aligned CTI credential. Covers collection planning, structured analytic techniques, intelligence reporting, legal/ethical frameworks, and geopolitical threat context. Practitioners who want the actual CREST CPTIA badge can sit the separate Pearson VUE exam after completing this course.

Mandiant Cyber Threat Intelligence Analysis — $250, Kryterion OLP proctored, 70% pass. The only genuinely proctored CTI cert in this stack. Mandiant/Google Cloud brand recognition adds credibility beyond the arcX credentials. Objectives mapped to NIST/NICE AN-ASA-001. 3-year renewal with CPE or retake. With arcX PTIA foundations established, MCTIA content is directly reinforced — cover IOC analysis, threat actor profiling, and cognitive bias mitigation before the exam.
